comments.author: @ShermanTW
comments.text: My interest in the Philippines has soared since discovering SB19 and P-pop in 2026. I can't believe it was off my radar for so long. The creativity and energy are mind-blowing! It’s a total cultural shock. Despite our close ties in ethnicity, culture, trade, and migrant workers, Taiwan-Philippines exchanges have stagnated for decades due to policies. Now, I’m rediscovering the country and even learning Tagalog. Thanks for the video!

comments.author: @romeocivilino6667
comments.text:
The Philippines is a Country of Archipelagoes. Palawan, being the Largest Province, is an Archipelago itself specifically the Largest Archipelagic Province in the Country, composed of several Island Groups:
First is the Main Island Group of Mainland Palawan, with Hundreds of Small & Medium-sized Islands beside, within and inside it's periphery.
Secondly, there's the Balabac Group of Islands in the Palawan's Southernmost Part, bordering the Balabac Strait, Sulu Sea and the West Philippine Sea.
Then, there is the Calamianes Archipelago(also known as Calamian Group of Islands) in the North of Palawan.
There's also the Cuyo Archipelago in the East, at the Middle of the Northern Side of Sulu Sea.
And Lastly, the Kalayaan Archipelago (Group of Islands), which is composed all of the Spratly Archipelago in the West Philippine Sea.

replies.author: @WhereonEarth4K
replies.text: Palawan as an archipelago within an archipelago — that layering is exactly what makes the Philippine geography one of the hardest things to fully communicate in a single film. Thank you for filling in what the frame couldn't hold.

comments.author: @hiderplanet
comments.text: These impossible places seriously make Earth feel like another planet. Every shot looked unreal.

comments.author: @ViktoriaSelenna
comments.text: the demands of jose rizal was not for independence. he wanted philippines to be a province of spain, in one of his demands he suggested  philippine representation in spanish courts. he refused bonifacio's offer to join the katipunan. mindanao was not a territory of spain, thats why rizal was "exciled" to dapitan (a dutch territory)..

replies.author: @WhereonEarth4K
replies.text: A fair point, and a debate historians still have. Rizal was indeed a reformist for most of his public life ...representation in the Cortes, the Philippines as a province with rights, rather than separation and he did decline to endorse the armed revolution when it came. The film tried to reflect exactly that, which is why we framed it as "the revolution he refused to lead." Thank you for adding the nuance.

comments.author: @necymamaril3735
comments.text: I grieve for my country for what it had become, despite the beauty , the rich natural resources which is being destroyed by wealthy and greedy companies and hard working talented Filipinos are driven away ( many have no choice) to foreign countries to enriched the corrupt, greedy politicians, by their remittances. How accurate the narrative is. "Birthday video calls, sometimes are heartbreaking, death /sickness in the family are worse to accept, when you are far away. Thank you for narrating, both the good and bad side of my country called the Philippines. We are resilient people, tenacious and family oriented and peace loving ,God fearing, qualities which are exploited and often times overlooked by our own government.

replies.author: @angeloherrera9883
replies.text: Its not over yet ! We can make a difference. God chose us lets be patient but alert 😊

replies.author: @WhereonEarth4K
replies.text: Thank you for this... and for watching to the end. The grief and the pride sit side by side in what you've written, which is exactly how the country feels from the outside looking in. The resilience, the closeness of family, the faith that fills the spaces nothing else can... those came through clearly to us in the making of this. The video calls and the distances are the hardest part of the story, and the part that rarely gets told. We're grateful you shared yours.
